svg.font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
svg.aria-label: Memtrace benchmark matrix versus ChromaDB, GitNexus, and CodeGrapherContext
svg.text: 5 benches · 4 systems · same machine, same datasets, same evaluators · 100% reproducible (MIT)

g.text: leaves the agent room to think
No LLM calls, no rate limits, no surprises
vs $10–50+ for Mem0 / Graphiti per repo
graph traversal that competitors can't reach
Bench #1 — Token economy (same data · per-cost lens)
Bench #2 — Intent retrieval (Django · NL → files · 100 queries · semantic workload)
Bench #3 — Graph queries · mempalace (~250 files · fair extractors, filtered)
Bench #3 — Graph queries · Django (~3,300 files · 13× larger corpus)
Bench #4 — Incremental freshness (50 edits × 4 adapters · live re-index)
Tally: Memtrace wins 19 of 26 axes, ties 2, trails 5 (Django NL semantics + reindex throughput).
6 modes: impact · novelty · recency · directional · compound · overview
frontend call → backend endpoint across repositories
named execution pipelines through the call graph
per-symbol scoring · hotspot detection on the graph
Try it: npm install -g memtrace → memtrace start
Auto-indexes your project · MCP-ready for Claude Code & Cursor v2.4+ · MIT-licensed
